items.statement: The system shall govern the lifecycle of an event from initial draft through its active life to conclusion.
The system shall maintain the relationships between an event and the members associated with it.
The system shall enable organizers to create and govern events and engage others to gather.
The system shall capture a member's intent to attend an event.
The system shall coordinate RSVP, comment, and reminder so that intent to attend is captured and nurtured into participation.
The system shall ensure that a member's expressed intent to attend reliably translates into participation.
The system shall reach the community about events and engagement regardless of which notification channel is used.
The system shall absorb the addition or change of notification channels without disrupting the organizing experience.
The system shall recognize members and organizers regardless of the chosen identity provider.
The system shall keep member and organizer relationships constant even as the underlying identity provider changes.
The system shall absorb the addition or change of identity providers without disrupting the organizing experience.
The system shall mediate between an event and external information about where it is held.
The system shall allow the place of an event to be sourced or changed without altering the event's structure.
The system shall absorb the addition or change of venue data sources without disrupting the organizing experience.
The system shall onboard a new notification channel, identity provider, or venue data source within one release cycle and at no more than 15% incremental engineering cost.
The system shall retain the enduring facts about an event and its relationships to members.
The system shall retain the state of members' RSVPs, comments, and reminders for events.
The system shall retain the enduring association between members or organizers and their recognized identity.
The system shall retain the association between an event and its resolved place.
